page: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an x86_64 architecture AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 2.0 threads per core operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, AES, VAES, and SHA_ni instructions. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but does not include any bundled local storage or integrated GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48as_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU stress tests and strong multi-core performance. Memory performance is strong for small-block cached read operations, though large-block read and write bandwidth falls into the average range. The instance exhibits strong capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations. For text and language processing, it delivers top-tier text generation speeds with small language models, though performance drops to average for medium models and is weak for large-model text generation.

This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads, software development, and database hosting. The absence of bundled local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads that prioritize high CPU performance and balanced memory allocation over local disk speed. Appropriate workloads include web serving, software compilation, database operations, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores) operating at 3.7 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance does not bundle local storage or GPUs, but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core execution, alongside strong database and software compilation capabilities. Memory bandwidth is strong for cached small blocks but average for larger uncached blocks. This server is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, database operations, software development, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suitable for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM, optimized for database and development workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-performance single-core execution for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core benchmark results
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
